text: In the era of digital transformation, blockchain technology, has emerged as a promising solution to solve the longstanding challenges faced by the healthcare sector such as interoperability, data integrity, inefficient resource utilization. Blockchain technology provides decentralized, immutable, digital ledger which can fundamentally change the healthcare systems at the global level. The purpose of this systematic review is to assess the impact of blockchain technology in healthcare by addressing the contemporary challenges in the field of medicine. Based on the PRISMA (Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses) guidelines, the most influential studies pertaining to blockchain technology in the field of medicine were screened and based on rigorous filtering criteria ten most impacting studies were chosen for the review. It is noticed from the conducted studies that the evolution of blockchain technology is going through two phases viz., the simulation phase and the implementation phase. The review depicts that the researchers are working towards the following applications in the field of medicine using blockchain technology such as increasing data integrity and security of data by implementing cryptographic algorithms, auditing medical supplies (such as drugs and Personal Protective Equipments (PPEs)), and increasing the secure data sharing among various healthcare stakeholders. In summary, the review captures the current scenario of the applications of blockchain technology in healthcare along with the many advantages and drawbacks associated with the implementation of blockchain technology in the various aspects of medicine and its clinical implementation.
